claim: MEK inhibitors are not effective in RAS-driven mouse models of cancer.

result.claim: MEK inhibitors are not effective in RAS-driven mouse models of cancer.
result.reasoning: The claim that MEK inhibitors are not effective in RAS-driven mouse models of cancer is refuted by multiple studies. Paper 33924486 (Credibility: 2.0/5, in_vitro/animal_study) demonstrates that dual MEK and ERK inhibition reduced tumor growth and increased progression-free survival in Kras-driven genetically engineered mouse models of NSCLC and PDAC. Specifically, it mentions "Dual MEK inhibitor plus ERK inhibitor therapy shows anti-tumor efficacy in an HrasG12V-driven autochthonous sarcoma model." Paper 51889704 (Credibility: 2.5/5, animal_study) shows that combination PI3K/MEK inhibition induced tumor regression in a mouse model of PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer. Furthermore, paper 22872574 (Credibility: 2.3/5, animal_study) found that combined treatment with a MEK inhibitor (AZD6244) and a PI3K/mTOR inhibitor (BEZ235) exhibited significant antitumor activity, including marked tumor regression and improved survival, in a RAS-driven melanoma GEMM and other breast cancer models. While some of these studies involve combination therapies rather than MEK inhibitors alone, they clearly demonstrate the effectiveness of MEK inhibition in RAS-driven mouse models when appropriately combined, directly contradicting the claim of general ineffectiveness.

evidence.text: NRF2 pathway inhibitors have shown selective cytotoxicity against leukemic cells in preclinical models.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: PI3K inhibition in combination with other agents has not been studied in the context of PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant cancer.
evidence.source: Combination PI3K/MEK inhibition promotes tumor apoptosis and regression in PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer

evidence.text: Adding MET inhibition to chemo-immunotherapy (anti-PD-L1) reshapes the tumor microenvironment by decreasing suppressive myeloid cell infiltration in SCLC mouse models.
evidence.source: MET pathway inhibition increases chemo-immunotherapy efficacy in small cell lung cancer.

evidence.text: In a study involving KrasG12D-driven genetically engineered mouse models of NSCLC and PDAC, dual MEK (Cobimetinib) and ERK (GDC-0994) inhibition reduced tumor growth and increased progression-free survival.
evidence.source: Sensitivity and Resistance of Oncogenic RAS-Driven Tumors to Dual MEK and ERK Inhibition.

evidence.text: Adding MET inhibition to chemo-immunotherapy (anti-PD-L1) reduces tumor growth in SCLC mouse models.
evidence.source: MET pathway inhibition increases chemo-immunotherapy efficacy in small cell lung cancer.

evidence.text: In SCLC mouse models, adding MET inhibition to chemo-immunotherapy (anti-PD-L1) reshapes the tumor microenvironment by decreasing suppressive myeloid cell infiltration.
evidence.source: MET pathway inhibition increases chemo-immunotherapy efficacy in small cell lung cancer.

evidence.text: Pan-PIM kinase inhibition did not effectively reduce viability of Burkitt lymphoma cell lines.
evidence.source: The role of histone kinases in controlling transcription in B cell lymphoma and leukaemia

evidence.text: In SCLC mouse models, adding MET inhibition to chemo-immunotherapy (anti-PD-L1) extends survival.
evidence.source: MET pathway inhibition increases chemo-immunotherapy efficacy in small cell lung cancer.

evidence.text: Inhibition of HDAC6 activity using a small-molecule inhibitor significantly improved the survival of mice bearing ARID1A-mutated tumours.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity.

evidence.text: The in vivo treatment of Chaetocin (0.25mg/kg) didn’t convey consistent anti-tumor effects in mice.
evidence.source: EXTH-82. TUMOR ORGANOID PREDICTED THERAPEUTIC RESPONSES TO NOVEL EPIGENETIC DRUGS VIA HIGH-THROUGHPUT SCREENING

evidence.text: Use of CSF1R inhibitors to target TAM has had very limited anti-tumor effects.
evidence.source: Cancer-Associated Fibroblasts Neutralize the Anti-tumor Effect of CSF1 Receptor Blockade by Inducing PMN-MDSC Infiltration of Tumors.

evidence.text: Aurora kinase inhibitors increase the magnitude and duration of EGFR inhibitor response in preclinical models.
evidence.source: Aurora kinase A drives the evolution of resistance to third-generation EGFR inhibitors in lung cancer.

evidence.text: The HDAC6 inhibitor ACY1215 improves the survival of ARID1A-mutated tumour bearing mice.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity.

evidence.text: Combination PI3K/MEK inhibition with NVP-BKM120 and PD-0325901 induced tumor regression in a mouse model of PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer.
evidence.source: Combination PI3K/MEK inhibition promotes tumor apoptosis and regression in PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer

evidence.text: CBD has been shown to inhibit the progression of several cancer types in animal models.
evidence.source: Cannabis sativa L. and Nonpsychoactive Cannabinoids: Their Chemistry and Role against Oxidative Stress, Inflammation, and Cancer

evidence.text: Inhibition of HDAC6 activity using a clinically applicable small molecule inhibitor significantly improved the survival of mice bearing ARID1A-mutated tumours.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity.

evidence.text: Dual MEK and ERK inhibition reduced tumor growth and increased progression-free survival in Kras-driven genetically engineered mouse models of NSCLC and PDAC.
